<findings>

[observation] [warning] High coupling (0.64) — 7 imports target "rex-prd-engine"
[observation] [warning] Low cohesion (0.36) — files are loosely related, consider splitting this zone
[observation] [warning] Cohesion 0.36 and coupling 0.64 confirm dual-fragility status — additions must satisfy the two-consumer rule and CLI-only content policy per CLAUDE.md satellite zone governance.
[observation] [info] With only 5 files the zone metrics are below the reliable-measurement threshold (5-file minimum); cohesion/coupling values reflect low internal relationship between a types file and two command handlers rather than structural decay.
[observation] [info] batch-types.ts is an entry point shared with rex-prd-engine; any change to batch type shapes will ripple into both the satellite zone and the main domain layer — treat it as a versioned contract.
[pattern] [warning] batch-types.ts is physically in packages/rex/src/analyze/ but zone-classified under chunked-review — moving the file to packages/rex/src/cli/commands/ would align physical location with zone ownership and eliminate the masked boundary crossing.
[relationship] [critical] rex-prd-engine imports from chunked-review (2 crossings), meaning the CLI satellite zone is not a pure leaf node — it introduces a load-order dependency from the domain layer onto a CLI command handler, which can cause initialization failures in bundled or ESM contexts.
[anti-pattern] [warning] batch-types.ts is physically located in packages/rex/src/analyze/ (rex-prd-engine territory) but zone-classified under chunked-review — this misclassification makes the 2 reverse imports from rex-prd-engine appear as an upward dependency when they may be the engine importing its own types. Move batch-types.ts to packages/rex/src/cli/commands/ or reclassify it under rex-prd-engine to resolve the ambiguity.
[suggestion] [info] loe-display-chunked-review.test.ts and loe-display-proposal-tree.test.ts share a 'loe-display' prefix but are classified in different zones — verify whether format-loe.ts (the apparent source for loe-display-proposal-tree.test.ts) should be co-classified with chunked-review-state.ts since both serve the LOE display subsystem, and document the split if intentional.
[suggestion] [warning] Zone "Chunked Review" (chunked-review) has critical risk (score: 0.64, cohesion: 0.36, coupling: 0.64) — requires refactoring before new feature development

</findings>

<insights>

- Low cohesion (0.36) — files are loosely related, consider splitting this zone
- High coupling (0.64) — 7 imports target "rex-prd-engine"
- Matches the 'chunked-review' satellite zone pattern documented in CLAUDE.md — cohesion 0.36 and coupling 0.64 place it firmly in dual-fragility territory requiring active governance.
- batch-types.ts acts as the shared contract between this satellite zone and the main Rex PRD Engine; any schema change there propagates broadly.
- Both production files and tests are co-located in this small zone; the zone is correctly NOT named with a '-tests' suffix since it contains production source files.
- Cohesion 0.36 and coupling 0.64 confirm dual-fragility status — additions must satisfy the two-consumer rule and CLI-only content policy per CLAUDE.md satellite zone governance.
- batch-types.ts is an entry point shared with rex-prd-engine; any change to batch type shapes will ripple into both the satellite zone and the main domain layer — treat it as a versioned contract.
- With only 5 files the zone metrics are below the reliable-measurement threshold (5-file minimum); cohesion/coupling values reflect low internal relationship between a types file and two command handlers rather than structural decay.
- chunked-review receives 2 return imports from rex-prd-engine (rex-unit → rex-cli), making it the only CLI satellite zone that the domain engine directly depends on — this is uniquely problematic because it means the CLI handler is load-order-coupled to the domain layer at runtime.
- The satellite zone owns batch-types.ts which is physically co-located inside the analyze/ directory of rex-prd-engine; the physical co-location masks the zone boundary and makes it appear as internal engine state rather than a shared contract.
- rex-prd-engine imports from chunked-review (2 crossings), meaning the CLI satellite zone is not a pure leaf node — it introduces a load-order dependency from the domain layer onto a CLI command handler, which can cause initialization failures in bundled or ESM contexts.
- batch-types.ts is physically in packages/rex/src/analyze/ but zone-classified under chunked-review — moving the file to packages/rex/src/cli/commands/ would align physical location with zone ownership and eliminate the masked boundary crossing.
- The 2 reverse imports from rex-prd-engine into chunked-review are almost certainly targeting batch-types.ts (the only non-CLI file in the zone) — if confirmed, reclassifying batch-types.ts as rex-prd-engine would reduce chunked-review to 2 CLI source files and 2 test files with no return edge, making it a clean satellite with correct metrics.
- chunked-review-state.ts likely maintains CLI-session state exclusively consumed by chunked-review.ts — as a single-consumer module it would fail the two-consumer rule if proposed as a new addition today; its presence as a legacy module represents a governance exception that is not documented anywhere.
- batch-types.ts is physically located in packages/rex/src/analyze/ (rex-prd-engine territory) but zone-classified under chunked-review — this misclassification makes the 2 reverse imports from rex-prd-engine appear as an upward dependency when they may be the engine importing its own types. Move batch-types.ts to packages/rex/src/cli/commands/ or reclassify it under rex-prd-engine to resolve the ambiguity.
- loe-display-chunked-review.test.ts imports from chunked-review-state.ts (not from chunked-review.ts), testing LOE display formatting functions — the 'loe-display' prefix encodes a functional concern (level-of-effort rendering) that is invisible from chunked-review-state.ts's module name. The functional sub-concern is documented only in the test file name, making chunked-review-state.ts's scope ambiguous to readers navigating from source to tests.
- loe-display-proposal-tree.test.ts exists in the same test directory as loe-display-chunked-review.test.ts but is not classified in the chunked-review zone — two test files share a 'loe-display' naming prefix but land in different zones, creating a split coverage surface for what appears to be a single LOE display subsystem.
- loe-display-chunked-review.test.ts and loe-display-proposal-tree.test.ts share a 'loe-display' prefix but are classified in different zones — verify whether format-loe.ts (the apparent source for loe-display-proposal-tree.test.ts) should be co-classified with chunked-review-state.ts since both serve the LOE display subsystem, and document the split if intentional.
- [call graph] 638 internal calls, 30 outgoing, 6 incoming (cohesion: 0.96, coupling: 0.04)

</insights>
